figures.caption: Fig. 1. Bilevel structure used in the solver. (A) The outer level optimizes belief-splitting coefficients α (parameterized by logits ϕ). (B) Given α, the inner level constructs the public belief tree (forward Bayes propagation), computes belief-averaged costs, and evaluates the induced tree of LQ saddle subgames via a tree-structured Riccati recursion, returning the root value and its gradient.

Fig. 3. Sample trajectories for the drone landing under attack case. A-B: Comparison between complete vs incomplete information games. P1 is better off in expectation when playing the incomplete-info game. Improvements for specific realizations are labeled in the plots. B-C: Comparison between re-solved vs offline policy when P2’s dynamics is stochastic. On average, resolving helps P1.
